entries.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
entries.description: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

entries.title: What to watch for after Jensen Huang’s Japan visit
entries.description: Jensen Huang left Tokyo with deals spanning Japan's entire tech ecosystem.

entries.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
entries.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast a shadow over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.

entries.title: ‘Odyssey’ director Christopher Nolan calls AI an obvious ‘Trojan horse’
entries.description: "Everybody knows the Greeks are inside."

entries.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
entries.description: Current AI, a nonprofit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat, and more.

entries.title: Kimi: Threat or menace?
entries.description: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

entries.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
entries.description: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

entries.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
entries.description: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

entries.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
entries.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

entries.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
entries.description: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.

entries.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
entries.description: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.

entries.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
entries.description: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of  turning AI into a business.

entries.title: Adobe camera app’s new feature will critique your photos using AI
entries.description: Adobe's Project Indigo can now remove all kinds of backgrounds from photos you snap using the app.

entries.title: YouTube clarifies policies around AI slop and upsetting videos
entries.description: YouTube has updated its monetization policies to more clearly define the kinds of AI-generated and low-quality videos that can’t earn ad revenue.
